# jplawdb2: Japanese Tax Law AI Database v3.0
# Direct-access principle: Read this document, no navigation needed.

## Design Principles

### 1. Cite-key → URL is deterministic. No search step required.
Any article you already know by cite_key (e.g., 措法66の6, 法基通2-1-1) can be fetched
in a single HTTP GET. The URL is mechanically derived from the cite_key:

  cite_key  →  split into (prefix → code) + (id, replace "の" with "_")
  措法66の6 →  text/sochi/66_6.txt
  法基通2-1-1 → text/hojin-kihon/2-1-1.txt

Do not search first. Construct the URL and fetch.

### 2. Granularity is article-level (条単位).
Each file contains exactly one article (条). Paragraphs (項), items (号), and
sub-items are embedded within that file as plain text markers ([p1], [p1-i1], etc.).
There is no finer-grained URL. Parse the body text after fetching.

### 3. meta/ is for discovery only — not for normal access.
Use meta/{code}.json or meta/catalog.json only when you do not know the article_id.
If you already know the cite_key, skip meta/ entirely and fetch the text file directly.

### 4. YAML front matter is ground truth.
Every .txt file begins with a YAML block. The fields cite_key, law_num, last_amended,
and status are authoritative. If your training data and the YAML disagree, trust the YAML.
Files with token_estimate > 10000 are flagged; plan for chunked reading if needed.
A full list of oversized articles is at: meta/oversized.json

### 5. Long articles are pre-split into chunks.
Articles exceeding ~10K tokens have been split into chunk files:

  text/{code}/{id}__c1.txt   (double underscore before "c")
  text/{code}/{id}__c2.txt
  ...

The main file ({id}.txt) is preserved in full and its YAML gains a "chunks: N" field.
Each chunk file has its own YAML: chunk_index, chunk_total, para_range, parent.
Split boundary is the top-level paragraph marker [pN]; sub-items stay together.
If you see "chunks: N" in the YAML, read the chunk files instead of the main file.
Full index of oversized articles: meta/oversized.json

### 6. Deleted provisions are excluded.
Articles marked "削除" (deleted) in the source law are not stored as individual files.
Their existence is recorded in meta/deleted_articles.json. A 404 for a known article
number likely means it was deleted, not that the DB is incomplete.

## Reading Protocol (follow this algorithm every time)

Step 1: Construct URL from cite_key
  prefix → code  (use the mapping tables below)
  article_id = article number with "の" replaced by "_"
  URL = https://jplawdb2.github.io/text/{code}/{article_id}.txt

Step 2: Fetch the URL
  200 → proceed to Step 3
  404 → article is deleted or not yet in DB (check meta/deleted_articles.json)

Step 3: Read the YAML front matter (first block between --- markers)
  If YAML contains "chunks: N" (N ≥ 2):
    → This article exceeds ~10K tokens. DO NOT read the body of this file.
    → Fetch chunk files instead:
         https://jplawdb2.github.io/text/{code}/{article_id}__c1.txt
         https://jplawdb2.github.io/text/{code}/{article_id}__c2.txt
         ...up to __cN.txt
    → Each chunk YAML contains: chunk_index, chunk_total, para_range
    → Read chunks sequentially. They cover the full article body.
  If YAML has no "chunks" field:
    → Article fits in one read. Use the body as-is.

Step 4: Parse body
  [pN]       = paragraph N (項)
  [pN-iM]    = item M of paragraph N (号)
  [pN-iM-sK] = sub-item K (subsidiary号 / イロハ)
  Body is plain text; no HTML or special encoding.

## URL Structure
https://jplawdb2.github.io/text/{code}/{id}.txt

## Law Codes ({tax-type}-{document-type} rule)
# Primary statutes
hojin       = Income Tax Act for Corporations (法人税法 / 法法)
hojin-rei   = Corporation Tax Act Enforcement Order (法人税法施行令 / 法令)
hojin-ki    = Corporation Tax Act Enforcement Regulations (法人税法施行規則 / 法規)
sochi       = Act on Special Measures Concerning Taxation (租税特別措置法 / 措法)
sochi-rei   = Special Taxation Measures Enforcement Order (措置法施行令 / 措令)
sochi-ki    = Special Taxation Measures Enforcement Regulations (措置法施行規則 / 措規)
shotoku     = Income Tax Act (所得税法 / 所法)
shotoku-rei = Income Tax Act Enforcement Order (所得税法施行令 / 所令)
shohi       = Consumption Tax Act (消費税法 / 消法)
sozoku      = Inheritance Tax Act (相続税法 / 相法)

## Administrative Guidance Codes (MUST use this table; do NOT guess)
hojin-kihon       = Corporation Tax Basic Circular (法人税基本通達 / 法基通)
shotoku-kihon     = Income Tax Basic Circular (所得税基本通達 / 所基通)
shohi-kihon       = Consumption Tax Basic Circular (消費税基本通達 / 消基通)
sochi-tsu-hojin   = Special Measures Circular, Corp. Tax (措置法通達(法人税関係) / 措通法)
sochi-tsu-shotoku = Special Measures Circular, Income Tax (措置法通達(所得税関係) / 措通所)
sozoku-kihon      = Inheritance Tax Basic Circular (相続税法基本通達 / 相基通)
tpg               = Transfer Pricing Administrative Guidelines (移転価格事務運営指針)

## Court Cases
text/hanrei/{alias}.txt    <- Major cases (with aliases)
text/hanrei/{case_id}.txt  <- All cases (numeric ID)
meta/hanrei.json           <- Search index (case name, articles, keywords)

## Filename Conversion Rules
"の" (no) -> underscore "_"
Example: 措法66の6     -> text/sochi/66_6.txt
Example: 法法22の2     -> text/hojin/22_2.txt
Example: 法法10の4の2の3 -> text/hojin/10_4_2_3.txt
Dashes in circulars are kept as-is:
Example: 法基通2-1-1   -> text/hojin-kihon/2-1-1.txt

## Frequently Used Direct Access
法法22      https://jplawdb2.github.io/text/hojin/22.txt
法法22の2   https://jplawdb2.github.io/text/hojin/22_2.txt
法法37      https://jplawdb2.github.io/text/hojin/37.txt
措法66の6   https://jplawdb2.github.io/text/sochi/66_6.txt
法基通2-1-1 https://jplawdb2.github.io/text/hojin-kihon/2-1-1.txt

## Common Mistakes (MUST READ)
X  Guess circular codes using romaji     -> USE the mapping table above
X  Include "の" directly in URLs         -> CONVERT to underscore
X  Access cases by article name directly -> SEARCH via meta/hanrei.json
X  Look for paragraph/item-level files   -> FIND within article-level file text
X  Access supplementary provisions       -> AVAILABLE in Phase 2+

## What This DB Cannot Do
- Full-text keyword search (use meta/*.json for index-based lookup)
- Historical versions of laws (current version only)
- Direct access to individual paragraphs or items (article-level only)

## Exploratory Access (when ID is unknown)
GET https://jplawdb2.github.io/meta/catalog.json   <- Full catalog
GET https://jplawdb2.github.io/meta/{code}.json    <- Article title index
GET https://jplawdb2.github.io/meta/hanrei.json    <- Court case index

## YAML Front Matter (every .txt file)
Each file starts with YAML front matter containing:
  schema_version, code, article_id, title, cite_key, law_num, last_amended, status
This metadata is machine-readable for structured extraction.
